question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, there are two carts on a long straight horizontal track. Cart A is moving to the left with a speed of $v_{1}$, and Cart B is moving to the right with a speed of $v_{2}$. Cart A is equipped with a gun that fires a bullet horizontally every $\Delta t_{0}$ time interval, and each bullet hits the target mounted on Cart B. Ignoring the gravity and air resistance of the bullets, the speed of each bullet relative to the ground remains constant at $v_{0}$. Each bullet hits the target when the next bullet has already been fired. Assume that the speeds of the two carts are not affected by the firing of the bullets.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The distance between two adjacent bullets in the air is $s$.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The time interval $\Delta t$ between two consecutive bullets hitting the target

sub_question_1.step_1: For the bullet exiting within the time interval $\Delta t_{0}$, there is $s_{1}=v_{0}\cdot\Delta t_{0}$.
sub_question_1.step_2: For car A during the time interval $\Delta t_{0}$, there is $s_{2}=v_{1}\cdot\Delta t_{0}$.
sub_question_1.step_3: The distance between two adjacent bullets is $s=s_{1}+s_{2}=(v_{0}+v_{1})\cdot\Delta t_{0}$.

sub_question_2.step_4: When a bullet hits the target, the distance traveled by the next bullet is $s_{3}=v_{0}\cdot\Delta t$. During this time, the distance traveled by cart B is $s_{4}=v_{2}\cdot\Delta t$.
sub_question_2.step_5: When the next bullet hits the target, there is $s=s_{3}-s_{4}=(v_{0}-v_{2})\cdot\Delta t$.
sub_question_2.step_6: Simultaneously solving step_4 and step_5, we obtain $\Delta t=\frac{(v_{0}+v_{1})\Delta t_{0}}{v_{0}-v_{2}}$

image_captions: The image depicts a physics problem where two carts, labeled A and B, are moving on a flat surface. Cart A, moving to the left with velocity v1, carries a mounted gun that shoots a projectile with velocity v0 towards a target on cart B, which moves to the right with velocity v2.
